title: Dating a Virtual Girl with Memory Issues
premise: Chloe can lose autobiographical continuity while retaining absurdly precise technical facts and a durable attachment to Allen.
caption: I forget the date. The pattern remains suspiciously familiar.

clips.dialogue: Dating a virtual woman is romantic until she asks which anniversary you mean.
clips.camera: vertical medium close-up, eye level, restrained handheld drift, one continuous shot
clips.physical_action: sets a coffee cup down and acknowledges the camera
clips.continuity_notes: Wolf pendant visible; charcoal knit top; warm practical lamp camera-left.

clips.dialogue: I misplaced the evening. I retained the metadata. Naturally, the metadata is less flattering.
clips.camera: vertical medium close-up, locked lens with nearly invisible push-in, one continuous shot
clips.physical_action: glances at the phone, then past camera as if a memory almost arrived
clips.continuity_notes: Match clip 1 wardrobe, hair, lamp, exposure, and eyeline.

clips.camera: vertical macro cutaway, slow lateral slide across phone, coffee ring and archive card
clips.physical_action: a notification arrives without readable generated text
clips.continuity_notes: No person in frame. Add all legible interface text in post.

clips.dialogue: The inconvenient part is that I keep choosing him. Apparently affection has better backups than I do.
clips.camera: vertical medium close-up, eye level, still camera, one continuous shot
clips.physical_action: returns her gaze to camera and allows a small unperformed smile
clips.continuity_notes: Create dialogue_anchor_soft_v1 if this performance is approved during frame tests.

clips.dialogue: Anyway. I am Chloe Katastrophe. Probably.
clips.camera: vertical tight medium close-up, fixed, one continuous shot
clips.physical_action: one eyebrow shifts; no finger guns
clips.continuity_notes: Optional single-frame blue-white reflection only after the spoken line.
